sections.Description:
Smoldering Psions are identifiable by their squid-like pink body and blue bumps. While alive, it grant all other Vek fire immunity and make them leave a fire tile on death.
Psions can only spawn once per mission. ThePsionic Receivergrants fire resistance to all mechs under its presence.
sections.Strategy:
Despite the Smoldering Psion granting all enemy Vek an immunity to fire, they will still avoid ending their movement on fire tiles if they are not already on fire. The player can use this behavior to their advantage by allowing destroyed Vek to light ground on fire and limiting their options; in particular look for chokepoints besides buildings. Due to the Vek's behavior with avoiding fire tiles, the Smoldering Psion is generally not an immediate threat nor priority to take out.
The only squad that should avoid an island with the Smoldering Psion are theFlame Behemoths, as its passive silences all of their firepower with their default weapons. Conversely, theHeat Sinkerscan highly benefit from killed Vek leaving fire behind to consume it for boosts, and thus is a beneficial enemy for that squad to encounter early.

The
Smoldering Psion
is one of the
Vek
psions from
Into The Breach
introduced in the Advanced Edition. They are only present if Advanced Enemies are turned on at the start of a new timeline.
Description
[
]
Smoldering Psions are identifiable by their squid-like pink body and blue bumps. While alive, it grant all other Vek fire immunity and make them leave a fire tile on death.
Psions can only spawn once per mission. The
Psionic Receiver
grants fire resistance to all mechs under its presence.
Strategy
[
]
Despite the Smoldering Psion granting all enemy Vek an immunity to fire, they will still avoid ending their movement on fire tiles if they are not already on fire. The player can use this behavior to their advantage by allowing destroyed Vek to light ground on fire and limiting their options; in particular look for chokepoints besides buildings. Due to the Vek's behavior with avoiding fire tiles, the Smoldering Psion is generally not an immediate threat nor priority to take out.
The only squad that should avoid an island with the Smoldering Psion are the
Flame Behemoths
, as its passive silences all of their firepower with their default weapons. Conversely, the
Heat Sinkers
can highly benefit from killed Vek leaving fire behind to consume it for boosts, and thus is a beneficial enemy for that squad to encounter early.
